author: nflaig
content: anyone else has a `glamsterdam-devnet-7` image to test against?

author: barnabasbusa
content: glamsterdam-devnet-7 testing thread

author: potuz
content: <@412614104222531604> have you tried enough slahed validators pre-fork so that the first proposers would be slashed?

author: barnabasbusa
content: that shouldn't matter right?

author: barnabasbusa
content: as the new logic should kick in after the fork + 2 epochs?

author: barnabasbusa
content: the fork epoch itself should be already set with the lookahead an epoch before

author: barnabasbusa
content: so the only time we would see such cases if we had fork + 1 epoch slot 0 with slashed validator

author: potuz
content:
Well, there are a gazillion bugs that can happen here:
1. Clients filter the slashed validators immediately at the fork in the first epoch of the fork (use Gloas logic by version of the state instead of using the pre-lookahead). 
2. Clients do not filter the slashed validators at fork+1, when computing the lookahead for fork + 2.

author: potuz
content: I don't know if we have fork tests for either of these behaviors

author: potuz
content: spectests I mean

author: nflaig
content: it's part of `state_transition` and we have spec tests, I don't understand how that can happen, the implementation of this is trivial

author: nflaig
content: the worst case is probably clients serving stale/wrong duties if they use separate caches for that

author: barnabasbusa
content: validator lookahead isn’t a new concept

author: barnabasbusa
content: we added that in fulu

author: jtraglia
content: Potuz is going to hide a lookahead bug in pyrsm just to prove y'all wrong 😁

author: potuz
content: what does this have to do with it? upgrade at the fork is the special thing. If clients call an update lookahead for Gloas right at the fork, then the next lookahead will be just wrong

author: potuz
content: why does everything I ask for need to be so controversial? I just asked for a test, it should be a nobrainer that testing scenarios is always better than just assuming the implementation is trivial and won't be flawed

author: potuz
content: I'm reviewing this exact code path in Prysm cause I suspected that I introduced the bug 1) above

author: jtraglia
content: I agree with you that we should a test for this. But an e2e (devnet, diamond) test.

author: nflaig
content: we did test this on devnet-6 already, and yes we should test this, but if there are bugs due to this I wouldn't be worried about the change itself but rather some more fundamental issue with the client design or the spec tests/specs in general

author: nflaig
content: it's probably mostly worth to look at how you serve duties and how the validator client caches them, I suspect this will likely be source of issues if any

author: potuz
content: this has nothing to do with duties in my concern. My concern is an actual consensus bug if the shuffling at the fork would point for the lookahead to have slashed validators in the first two epochs after Gloas and clients treating them differently, both epoch transitions last Fulu -> First Gloas and First Gloas -> Second Gloas are my worry and I think we should have tests for.

author: potuz
content: I did audit Prysm and Prysm seems fine on both epoch transitions AFAICT

author: potuz
content: I do not think devnet 6 forked with enough slashed validators to guaratee slashed validators in the first 2 epochs post Gloas?

author: potuz
content: This is kinda crazy to test on devnet, needs to be Kurtosis or a spectest

author: nflaig
content: why do you wanna specifically test this on the devnet? shouldn't we just add more spec tests for this?

author: potuz
content:
eg

For 1) a Fork spectest testvector with upgrade_to_gloas with prepared tons of slashed validators and check that there are slashed validators in the first two epochs of the lookahead
For 2) an epoch transition on Gloas where the pre-state has slashed validators in the lookahead (this can happen in the first couple of epochs or after a slashing) and the second half of the lookahead does not have any after processing the epoch.

author: potuz
content: I specifically **do not** want this tested on the devnet
